mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding household income and consumption demand, which translates into higher expected corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of non-recession months, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is held below 0.25%, persistent monetary accommodation suppresses short-term discount rates and credit risk premiums, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, the average 60-day forward S&P 500 return is negative (t-test p < 0.05), or positive returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in industrial production (INDPRO) and a fall in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signal a coordinated recovery in output capacity and labor demand, jointly lifting earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint observations of rising INDPRO and falling UNRATE fail to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in a bivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, strong retail sales (RSAFS) signal rapid consumer demand normalization that supports near-term revenue beats and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: During post-shock recovery months, elevated RSAFS is followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations, or the mean forward return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1,400 thousand units signal robust residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials and consumer durables, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST levels above 1,400 thousand are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered months, or the mean forward return is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) alongside a near-zero federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signal mounting inflationary pressure that is not offset by monetary tightening, eroding real corporate margins and driving negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint observations of rising CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS below 0.25% fail to produce negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An expanding industrial production index signals rising manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which supports near-term earnings momentum and generates positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Rising INDPRO is followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of months, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, quarterly real GDP (GDPC1) rebounding above $18,500 billion signals aggregate demand normalization and inventory restocking, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 levels above $18,500 billion are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered quarters, or the mean forward return is not positive.
